description_long: This object consists of a group of four round, reddish-brown plant pots, each with a classic tapered cylindrical shape and a slightly flared rim at the top. The pots appear to be made of terracotta or a similar ceramic material, with a matte, slightly rough surface texture. The interiors are visible and appear empty, with a dark, shadowed look suggesting depth. The pots vary in size, with two larger and two smaller ones, arranged in a loose cluster. The exterior surfaces show subtle variations in color and texture, giving a natural, weathered appearance. These pots are typical of those used for outdoor gardening or urban landscaping.
description: A group of four reddish-brown terracotta plant pots of varying sizes with classic tapered shapes and flared rims, featuring a matte, slightly rough surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four pots, with two larger pots at the back and two smaller ones in front, highlighting their size differences and classic shapes.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth and height of the largest pot, with the smaller pots partially obscured and arranged to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with the two largest pots in the foreground, showing their wide openings and the smaller pots behind them.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying the group arrangement, with the largest pot on the left and the smaller pots more clearly separated on the right.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used in urban gardens, patios, or sidewalk landscaping
